sections.heading: This Prudential Standard is made under:
sections.title: Prudential Standard authority basis
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Prudential Standard is enacted under three specific sections of Australian legislation: the Banking Act 1959, the Insurance Act 1973, and the Life Insurance Act 1995. It establishes the legal foundation for the standard by referencing the relevant regulatory acts that authorize its implementation.

requirements.text: This Prudential Standard is made under: section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959 (Banking Act); section 32 of the Insurance Act 1973 (Insurance Act); and section 230A of the Life Insurance Act 1995 (Life Insurance Act).

sections.source_quote: This Prudential Standard is made under: section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959 (Banking Act); section 32 of the Insurance Act 1973 (Insurance Act); and section 230A of the Life Insurance Act 1995 (Life Insurance Act).

sections.heading: This Prudential Standard applies to all ‘APRA-regulated institutions',1 defined
sections.title: APRA-regulated institutions scope
sections.summary: This section defines which financial institutions are covered by the Prudential Standard. It includes authorized deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), foreign ADIs, non-operating holding companies under the Banking Act, general insurers, Category C insurers, non-operating holding companies under the Insurance Act, and life companies including friendly societies and eligible foreign life insurance companies.

requirements.text: This Prudential Standard applies to all ‘APRA-regulated institutions', defined as: all authorised deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), including foreign ADIs, and non-operating holding companies authorised under the Banking Act (authorised banking NOHCs); all general insurers, including Category C insurers, non-operating holding companies authorised under the Insurance Act (authorised insurance NOHCs) and parent entities of Level 2 insurance groups; and all life companies, including friendly societies and eligible foreign life insurance companies (EFLICs), and non-operating holding companies registered under the Life Insurance Act (registered life NOHCs).

sections.source_quote: This Prudential Standard applies to all ‘APRA-regulated institutions', defined as: all authorised deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), including foreign ADIs, and non-operating holding companies authorised under the Banking Act (authorised banking NOHCs); all general insurers, including Category C insurers, non-operating holding companies authorised under the Insurance Act (authorised insurance NOHCs) and parent entities of Level 2 insurance groups; and all life companies, including friendly societies and eligible foreign life insurance companies (EFLICs), and non-operating holding companies registered under the Life Insurance Act (registered life NOHCs).

sections.heading: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential
sections.title: Defined terms reference
sections.summary: This section states that terms defined in other Prudential Standards (APS 001, GPS 001, LPS 001) will be in bold for the first time they appear in this standard. It clarifies how terminology is handled across different regulatory frameworks.

requirements.text: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential Standard APS 001 Definitions (APS 001), Prudential Standard GPS 001 Definitions (GPS 001) or Prudential Standard LPS 001 Definitions appear in bold the first time they are used in this Prudential Standard.

sections.source_quote: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential Standard APS 001 Definitions (APS 001), Prudential Standard GPS 001 Definitions (GPS 001) or Prudential Standard LPS 001 Definitions appear in bold the first time they are used in this Prudential Standard.

sections.heading: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard:
sections.title: Group and Head definitions
sections.summary: This section defines key terms such as 'group', 'Head of a group', 'Level 2 group', and 'Level 2 Head' for the purpose of business continuity management. It specifies the structure of institutional groups and their leadership roles under the Prudential Standard.

requirements.text: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard: ‘group' means a Level 2 group or a Level 3 group, as relevant; ‘Head of a group' means a Level 2 Head or Level 3 Head, as relevant; ‘Level 2 group' means the entities that comprise: (a) Level 2 as defined in APS 001; or (b) a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001; ‘Level 2 Head' means: (a) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is not a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC or another ADI, that ADI; (b) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC, that authorised banking NOHC; or (c) the parent entity of a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001.

sections.source_quote: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard: ‘group' means a Level 2 group or a Level 3 group, as relevant; ‘Head of a group' means a Level 2 Head or Level 3 Head, as relevant; ‘Level 2 group' means the entities that comprise: (a) Level 2 as defined in APS 001; or (b) a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001; ‘Level 2 Head' means: (a) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is not a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC or another ADI, that ADI; (b) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC, that authorised banking NOHC; or (c) the parent entity of a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001.

sections.heading: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the
sections.title: Major disruption notification
sections.summary: This section requires the Head of a group to notify APRA if an institution experiences a major disruption that could materially impact its risk profile or financial soundness, with an exception if another APRA-regulated institution within the group has already notified APRA.

requirements.text: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness, except where an APRA-regulated institution within the group has otherwise notified APRA of that information.

sections.source_quote: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness, except where an APRA-regulated institution within the group has otherwise notified APRA of that information.

sections.heading: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report
sections.title: Business continuity risk management
sections.summary: This section mandates that APRA-regulated institutions must identify, assess, manage, mitigate, and report on potential business continuity risks to ensure they can meet financial and service obligations to depositors, policyholders, and other stakeholders.

requirements.text: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report on potential business continuity risks to ensure that the institution is able to meet its financial and service obligations to its depositors, policyholders and other stakeholders.

sections.source_quote: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report on potential business continuity risks to ensure that the institution is able to meet its financial and service obligations to its depositors, policyholders and other stakeholders.

sections.heading: The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken
sections.title: Board must integrate business continuity risks into risk management
sections.summary: The Board must ensure business continuity risks and controls are incorporated into the institution's overall risk management strategy and included in the risk management declaration submitted to APRA. This requirement applies to APRA-regulated financial institutions to maintain robust risk oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that business continuity risks be systematically integrated into risk management, which is critical for DTIAs to assess how disruptions might affect cross-border operations and regulatory compliance.

requirements.text: The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken into account as part of the institution's risk management strategy
The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken into account when completing a risk management declaration required to be provided to APRA

sections.source_quote: The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken into account as part of the institution's risk management strategy and when completing a risk management declaration required to be provided to APRA.

sections.heading: BCM is a whole-of-business approach that includes policies, standards and
sections.title: Business continuity management as whole-of-business approach
sections.summary: Business continuity management requires institutions to establish policies, standards, and procedures that ensure critical business operations can be maintained or recovered during disruptions. This approach aims to minimize financial, legal, regulatory, and reputational consequences from operational interruptions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines business continuity as a whole-of-business process, which is essential for DTIAs to evaluate how disruptions might impact cross-border data flows and recovery strategies.

requirements.text: BCM is a whole-of-business approach that includes policies, standards and procedures for ensuring that critical business operations can be maintained or recovered in a timely fashion, in the event of a disruption

sections.source_quote: BCM is a whole-of-business approach that includes policies, standards and procedures for ensuring that critical business operations can be maintained or recovered in a timely fashion, in the event of a disruption.

sections.heading: When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider:
sections.title: BIA must consider disruption scenarios and impacts
sections.summary: When conducting a Business Impact Analysis (BIA), institutions must evaluate plausible disruption scenarios over time, the operational downtime for critical functions, the impact on depositors/policyholders, and the financial, legal, regulatory, and reputational consequences of disruptions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires detailed analysis of disruption impacts, which is vital for DTIAs to assess how business continuity risks might affect cross-border data transfers and recovery processes.

requirements.text: When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider plausible disruption scenarios over varying periods of time
When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider the period of time for which the institution could not operate without each of its critical business operations

When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider the extent to which a disruption to the critical business operations might have a material impact on the interests of depositors and/or policyholders of the institution

When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider the financial, legal, regulatory and reputational impact of a disruption to the institution's critical business operations over varying periods of time

sections.source_quote: When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider: (a) plausible disruption scenarios over varying periods of time; (b) the period of time for which the institution could not operate without each of its critical business operations; (c) the extent to which a disruption to the critical business operations might have a material impact on the interests of depositors and/or policyholders of the institution; and (d) the financial, legal, regulatory and reputational impact of a disruption to the institution's critical business operations over varying periods of time.

sections.heading: An APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA as soon as possible and no
sections.title: Notify APRA of major disruptions within 24 hours
sections.summary: APRA-regulated institutions must report major disruptions that could materially impact their risk profile or financial stability to APRA within 24 hours, including details about the disruption, actions taken, expected effects, and timeline for recovery. They must also notify APRA when normal operations resume.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires timely reporting of disruptions, which is crucial for DTIAs to understand how cross-border data transfers might be affected by operational interruptions.

requirements.text: An APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA as soon as possible and no later than 24 hours after the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness
The APRA-regulated institution must explain to APRA the nature of the disruption, the action being taken, the likely effect and the timeframe for returning to normal operations
The APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA when normal operations resume

sections.source_quote: An APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA as soon as possible and no later than 24 hours after the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness.

sections.heading: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard
sections.title: APRA may adjust or exclude prudential requirements
sections.summary: APRA has the authority to modify or exclude specific requirements within this Prudential Standard for regulated institutions, subject to relevant legislation such as the Banking Act, Insurance Act, and Life Insurance Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section allows APRA to tailor business continuity requirements, which may impact DTIAs by adjusting cross-border data transfer obligations for specific institutions.

requirements.text: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard in relation to an APRA-regulated institution

sections.source_quote: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard in relation to an APRA-regulated institution.
